It's a killer.<br /><br />I ordered this set because I wanted shampoo and conditioner that were sulfate-free, silicone-free, etc. and they do adhere to those requirements of the Curly Girls out there. BUT the conditioner left my hair feeling SO HEAVY.<br /><br />Maybe they don't use silicones, but these products leave SOMETHING in your hair to &#34;protect your color&#34; - and holy crap did it weigh my hair down. I can usually go 3 - 4 days without washing but with this stuff I had to wash daily because my hair looked a greasy by the end of the day. And my curls? Nowhere to be found. Maybe someone with more intense curls will have better luck with this, but this took my 2B/3B hair down to a 2A at its fullest and stick straight most everywhere else. Massive frizz. No body. No shape.<br /><br />This stuff is wicked expensive and does a way worse job than the incredibly affordable Tresemme Perfectly (Un)done line which is also sulfate and silicone free. I found it at my local drug store for $5/large bottle which is half the cost from Amazon to boot.<br /><br />$50 for a hot mess compared to what I get from a bargain brand is a wicked bummer. Maybe my roommate's 3B-3C locks will do better with it. 3.0	Ok shampoo for color, not great for scalp health	I love the Nexxus humectress line, so after I dyed my hair blonde I decided to try the Nexxus color care line. I think this shampoo/conditioner set has many pros, for example my hair color was well preserved, it left my hair feeling decently soft after using, however I found that it leaves residue that doesn't quite come off, and my hair always felt greasy and sometimes pretty itchy on day 2. I noticed if I scratched my scalp at that point my nails would smell pretty gross and sour.  I used this set for approx 2-3 months and only when I came home and used a different conditioner I realized it was the Nexxus shampoo causing the issue (I had thought it was just irritation from the purple shampoo I was using). The itch and stink pretty much went away after I switched to a different shampoo. Nexxus Color Assure Shampoo and Conditioner are salon crafted for color treated hair, nourishing while helping to protect color vibrancy. These innovative formulas gently cleanse and reawaken color treated hair to give you vibrant hair that moves beautifully. Use this shampoo and conditioner together with the rest of the Nexxus Color Assure Vibrancy Retention Collection to protect your hair color for longer. Nexxus Color Assure Shampoo and Conditioner for Color Treated Hair Color Assure Enhance Color Vibrancy Collection is our premiere system for all your colored hair needs.","Nexxus New York Salon recommends:Enriched with White Orchid, Nexxus Color Assure Shampoo and Conditioner for color treated hair replenish nutrients and help maintain color vibrancy.Step 1: Cleanse hair with Nexxus Color Assure Shampoo. 5.0	I put 5 stars so this would be a higher up response. But it’s not even a 1	I read a lot of reviews , good and bad. I was skeptical because of mixed opinions, however I convinced myself to go ahead and try it. A lot of the reviews that were bad were about color stripping and broken pumps. My order was packaged perfectly and my bottles were not broken. However, this is by far one of the worst products I’ve ever used on my hair. I don’t know if it’s a knock off, or what but it’s bad. 4.0	Amazing!	Okay so I have never dyed my hair before until just recently and I had no idea how to care for my hair afterwards. My friend asked her friend, who’s going to cosmetology school, what I should use for my hair. And full disclosure: my hair is insane! It’s super thin, I have a very small amount of hair (have to use extensions for an updo), and it loves to stay super dry at the ends and super oily at the top. 1.0	Watery shampoo, conditioner does not rinse out well	I am doubting the product I received was authentic. One, the shampoo is very watery, making it hard to use. Second, the conditioner does not rinse out cleanly. I have used this three times and I thought the first time I must have forgotten to rinse my hair in the shower it was so bad. Nope.
